title: Minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval as an early go/no-go kill-gate (handful of fixtures)
context: A full eval harness (public-official-guide-eval-018) runs in M4, but the grounded-vs-blank-slate thesis needs an early kill-gate at M1: a small fixture set that checks whether memory-grounded+cited output trends ahead of blank-slate output. If grounded+cited does not at least trend ahead on this small set, HIGH-tier content work (M2) pauses for review rather than waiting for M4 to discover the thesis is failing. This early gate is intentionally lightweight -- a handful of fixtures -- and re-run in M2 on real cited content.
objective: Build a minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval on a small fixture set that runs the office-memory faculties in grounded+cited mode and blank-slate mode, scores the delta, and gates the M1 exit: if grounded+cited does not trend ahead, M2 content investment pauses.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs the office-memory faculties grounded+cited vs. blank-slate on a small fixture set (minimum 5 fixture scenarios)
Reports the delta as a scored go/no-go: if grounded+cited does not at least trend ahead on accuracy and citation, the eval output explicitly flags a go/no-go HOLD
Integrated into the M1 definition of done: M2 content work does not begin if the eval flags HOLD
Designed to be re-run on real cited content in M2 (the same harness, more fixtures)
output: A merged PR with the minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval harness on a small fixture set, reporting a scored go/no-go delta to gate M2 content investment.
